name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: Cash America Pawn Houston, Texas — FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the U.K.,...
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest international pawn store chain in the world. Founded and continuously expanded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers, the company has grown to operate more than 3,300 retail pawn locations spanning 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador. The company employs approximately 22,000 people across these markets and is listed on the Standard & Poor's MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index.

FirstCash's core business model revolves around pawn loans—non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property—and retail sales of merchandise. Customers can obtain fast pawn loans by bringing items of value to any location. The company buys and sells jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments, and general merchandise. Additionally, FirstCash offers layaway services with a 10% down payment option, gold and precious metal buying services, and a store locator tool to find nearby locations with real-time inventory.

What distinguishes FirstCash is its scale and geographic reach as the largest pawn operator globally, combined with its integration of technology-driven point-of-sale solutions through its wholly owned subsidiary AFF, which provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions across 15,000+ merchant partners nationwide. The company's multi-country presence and diverse merchandise buying approach differentiate it from smaller regional pawn operators.

For consumers, FirstCash provides an accessible option for obtaining fast cash without credit checks or lengthy approval processes—ideal for immediate liquidity needs. However, pawn loans require surrendering collateral, carry interest charges, and operate on a secured loan model where failure to repay results in forfeiture of the pledged item. The company's scale is both a strength (accessibility and convenience) and a reflection of reliance on financially vulnerable populations.
pros: Massive network of 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, D.C., U.K., Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador for convenient access
Fast same-day pawn loans with no credit check required—ideal for immediate cash needs
Non-recourse loans meaning you're not personally liable for loan balance if item is forfeited
Buys almost anything of value including gold, silver, platinum, jewelry, electronics, tools, and instruments with instant cash payment
Layaway service available with only 10% down payment for deferred purchases
Largest and most established pawn operator globally with 22,000+ employees and stock market listing
Accepts a wide variety of merchandise, increasing likelihood of getting a loan regardless of what collateral you have
cons: Losing collateral is a real risk—if you cannot repay the pawn loan, you permanently lose the pledged item with no recourse
Pawn loans carry interest charges that make the total cost of borrowing higher than credit-based alternatives; rates not disclosed on website
Layaway service charges 10% down with no detail on total cost, potentially expensive for budget-conscious shoppers
As a for-profit public company, FirstCash targets financially vulnerable populations; the business model relies on collateral seizure
No information provided on website regarding loan terms, repayment periods, interest rates, or fees—transparency is limited
best_for: Consumers needing immediate cash who own items of value (jewelry, electronics, tools) and cannot access traditional credit
People with no credit history or poor credit who cannot qualify for personal loans or credit cards
Individuals seeking short-term loans who are confident they can repay and reclaim their collateral
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for individuals with immediate liquidity needs who own valuable collateral and cannot access traditional credit. The main caveat is that pawn loans require surrendering personal property as security, and failure to repay results in permanent loss of that item—making this appropriate only for those confident in their ability to repay within the loan term.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property with fast approval
Retail sales of jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments
Gold, silver, and precious metal buying with instant cash payment
Merchandise layaway plans with 10% down payment
Store locator with real-time inventory browsing
Retail lease-to-own and payment solutions through AFF subsidiary at 15,000+ merchant partners
Merchandise lending library (customers can pawn and retrieve items repeatedly)
Buy and sell services for general merchandise
